commit.message:
Remove .env from tracking (keep locally)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Full MLO-style natural language parsing engine

Date/time parser (parseDateTimeExpr):
- Multi-word expressions: "in 3 days 4pm", "next Friday 9am", "in 2 months"
- Named: today, tomorrow, now + weekday names (mon..sun, full names)
- Month names: Jan 26, August 26th, Jan26 combined, Nov 26 08 (year)
- Relative: in N d/w/m/y, in N h/min, combined "in 1 month 2 weeks 1 day"
- Weekday refinement: "in 3 weeks Fri" → +3w then snap to Friday
- Time: 3pm, 3p, 15:10, 11am, at 3pm, separate "3 pm" tokens
- "today in 1h 25min" → today + time offset from now
- "in 30 min" → pure time-only offset

Inline date detection (findDateBoundary):
- Right-to-left scan: consumes date tokens from end of line
- "Call Jim tomorrow 4pm" → caption: "Call Jim", due: tomorrow 4pm
- "Send report in 3 days" → caption: "Send report", due: in 3 days
- "in"/"next"/"at" only consumed when followed by valid date context

Reminder parsing (parseReminderWords):
- "remind N min in advance" / "before" / "ahead" → due time minus offset
- "remind me tomorrow 2pm" → explicit datetime
- "remind me" → same as due date/time

Quoted captions: "Call Jim" tomorrow → caption="Call Jim", rest parsed

New switches:
- -fl<FlagName>: assign flag
- -star, -* : starred
- -s, -start: parsed date → start field only
- -d, -due: parsed date → due field only
- @TagName: tag alias (alongside #TagName)

Both syntaxes work simultaneously:
  old: "Buy groceries !urgent d:tomorrow #work"
  new: "Call Jim tomorrow 4pm remind 10 min in advance"
  mix: "Send report next Tuesday -d remind me @work"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move token priming from aggregator to enricher, use Solscan create_tx

Replaces the old prime strategy (aggregator → producer → N sigs per token)
with a lightweight approach: enricher fetches create_tx from Solscan
token/meta/multi and cascades 1 signature per token directly to
decoder+detailer, bypassing the producer entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rapid Input, parsing engine, Alt+Enter inline parsing

Parsing engine (src/lib/parsing.ts):
- Token syntax: !FlagName, #TagName, s:DATE, d:DATE, r:DATE, * (starred)
- Flag/tag matching: case-insensitive, partial prefix match for flags
- Date formats: today/tod, tomorrow/tom, weekday names (mon/tuesday/...),
  nextweek/nw, relative (+3d +2w +1m), ISO (2024-01-15), DD/MM/YY
- Indentation-aware tree builder: tabs=4sp equiv, normalised to depth levels
- flattenNodes() helper for display

Rapid Input dialog (Ctrl+Shift+I or 📋 Rapid button):
- Large textarea for pasting multi-line indented task lists
- Live preview panel (right side): shows parsed hierarchy with flag dots,
  tag chips, and colour-coded date badges (s: green, d: red, r: gold)
- "Apply parsing" checkbox: toggle token extraction on/off
- Parent task picker: searchable dropdown showing full task tree with
  indentation; select any task as the parent, or leave at Root
- Import button (or Ctrl+Enter): creates all tasks in order, expands
  parent, navigates to Outline
- Esc to close

Alt+Enter in inline edit (TaskRow):
- While editing a task caption, press Alt+Enter to apply parsing
- Extracts flag, tags, start/due dates, reminder, starred flag from
  the typed text; updates task with all parsed fields; closes editor

feat: migrate to Talos (#234)

## Summary

- Migrate all app charts from inline `hostPath` to explicit PV/PVC
pattern (`config/pv.yaml` + `existingClaim`)
- Fix MetalLB L2 ARP on Talos single-node: remove
`exclude-from-external-load-balancers` label via
`allowSchedulingOnControlPlanes: true`
- Deploy Traefik on `.201`, pin shared services (qbittorrent, plex,
threadfin, fileflows) to `.200` with `allow-shared-ip`
- Migrate all services with Traefik ingress to `ClusterIP`
- Add NFS PVs for shared media (nebula) across radarr, sonarr, bazarr,
plex, jellyfin, fileflows, syncthing
- Add local-path-provisioner for dynamic storage at
`/var/local/local-path`
- Remove rsnapshot (wdata source no longer applicable)

## Apps migrated

- **media**: plex, radarr, sonarr, bazarr, tautulli, overseerr,
jellyfin, threadfin
- **downloads**: qbittorrent, prowlarr
- **automation**: n8n, syncthing, apprise, fileflows, backrest
- **development**: code-server

## Test plan

- [ ] Sync each ArgoCD application and verify pods start
- [ ] Verify PVCs bind to correct PVs
- [ ] Verify plex accessible at `192.168.50.200:32400`
- [ ] Verify qbittorrent accessible at `192.168.50.200:8112`
- [ ] Verify web apps accessible via Traefik ingress on `.201`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

-->

Lighter theme, reminders, Use time, cross-view selection

Theme:

Dates:

  - Double-click row → navigate to task in Outline
  - Checks every 30s; dismiss/snooze state persisted in localStorage

Cross-view navigation:
- Clicking any task in a View selects it in the shared selection store
- Double-clicking a task in any View switches to Outline tab, expands
  all ancestor nodes, scrolls to + briefly highlights the task row
- navigateToOutline() helper in tasks store

commit.message:
Pin bcrypt==3.2.2 to fix passlib compatibility

passlib 1.7.4 detect_wrap_bug() uses a >72 byte password, which bcrypt 4.x
now rejects with ValueError. Pinning to 3.2.2 resolves the startup crash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix backtest stuck: use sync classify/generate_signal in thread pool

Root cause found: loop.run_until_complete() on async coroutines inside
asyncio.to_thread() was causing deadlocks. The regime_engine.classify()
and signal_engine.generate_signal() are async def but never actually
await anything — they're purely CPU-bound.

Fix:
- Add classify_sync() to RegimeEngine (calls _classify_impl directly)
- Add generate_signal_sync() to SignalEngine (calls _generate_signal_impl)
- Backtester._process_bars() uses sync versions directly in thread pool
- No event loop needed in the thread — clean synchronous execution
- Added progress logging every 200 bars

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Compress adjacent [ProtocolSupport] ranges in PacketPostProcessor

Adjacent non-null history ranges are now merged into a single
[ProtocolSupport] attribute, same logic as CompressPacketIds.
Example: [ProtocolSupport(first, 770)] + [ProtocolSupport(771, last)]
→ [ProtocolSupport(first, last)]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(ship): close all production blockers for v1.0.0

Blocker 1 — FloodSub scale cap:
  - settings.py: new `max_swarm_size` field (default 30, soft cap)
  - Warns at startup when data_shard_total > max_swarm_size so operators
    know the O(N²) cost before deploying, not during training.

Blocker 2 — Free relay (no VPS cost):
  - fly.toml: one-file fly.io config — free tier (3 VMs, 160 GB/month)
  - scripts/deploy_relay_fly.sh: one-command relay deploy (creates app,
    sets key seed, deploys, extracts multiaddr, optionally commits to manifests)
  - make relay-fly: Makefile shortcut
  - JOIN.md: free relay options (fly.io, ngrok, Tailscale, Oracle Cloud)
  - docker/docker-compose.relay.yml was already present; fly.io is the zero-cost path

Blocker 3 — HierarchicalAggregation fast-fail:
  - settings.py: new `validate_hierarchical_strategy` model_validator raises
    ValueError at startup if aggregation_strategy='hierarchical' — prevents
    silent fallback-to-flat at large scale
  - strategy.py: HierarchicalAggregation.aggregate() now raises
    NotImplementedError (defence-in-depth, settings check is primary)

Blocker 4 — CI/CD:
  - ci.yml: trigger on all branches (was: only main/dev)
  - ci.yml: first pass uses --no-cov (tests must pass), second pass with
    coverage uploads artifact — coverage threshold never hides test failures

Bonus — 2-node Docker sim for resource-constrained machines:
  - docker/docker-compose.2node.yml: 2 GB RAM / 1 CPU per container
  - make sim-2 / sim-2-down / sim-2-logs targets

131 tests passing, mypy clean, ruff clean.

commit.message:
fix: strip markdown backticks from Haiku thinking response, remove fallback

Root cause: when Extended Thinking is enabled, Haiku wraps JSON output
in markdown code fences. The regex parser could not find the JSON array
inside the backticks, causing parse failure and 502.

Changes:
- Strip markdown code fences before JSON parsing (classJson + keywords)
- Remove unnecessary fallback-without-thinking code path
- Simplify API call: always use thinking with 16000 max_tokens budget
- Add response structure logging (block types, lengths, stop_reason)
- Add warning when no JSON array found in response

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Restructure household savings: 5 components, total ≈ 71 trln

Replace hh_deposits (only term deposits, ~42 trln) with hh_savings
covering all household savings components from CBR Excel files:
- Term deposits (~46 trln) from monetary_agg.xlsx
- Savings accounts with interest (~12 trln) from monetary_agg.xlsx
- Current accounts (~7 trln) from monetary_agg.xlsx
- Foreign currency deposits (~3 trln) from monetary_agg.xlsx
- Escrow accounts (~7 trln) from 02_01_Funds_all.xlsx
Total ≈ 71 trln (from funds_all aggregate figure).

Switched data source from unreliable CBR DataService API to
official CBR Excel files (02_01_Funds_all.xlsx + monetary_agg.xlsx).

commit.message:
fix(audit): harden compressor, gossip, aggregator, and ban-list

Security and correctness fixes from post-Phase-8 audit:

C1 (compressor): validate numel/shape/k_count/index bounds in _decode_sparse
  before allocating the dense tensor — prevents OOM from malicious peers.
H1 (peer_selector): reset rejection counters on ban expiry so peers get a
  clean slate and aren't immediately re-banned on next round.
H3 (averaging): track original dtype and cast weighted sum back after
  fp32 accumulation — preserves fp16/bf16 memory layout on GPU.
M2 (gossip): remove per-chunk _evict_stale_transfers(); timer-driven
  _eviction_loop() (every 30s) handles cleanup at O(1) cost.
M3 (gossip): cap sender_id length at 256 bytes before parsing to prevent
  CPU/memory waste from crafted headers.
timeout: replace O(N) set comprehension with O(1) _seen_peer_ids set.

Also adds AUDIT.md, architecture.md, banner asset, and test_audit_fixes.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: Jupiter 429 rate limit — backoff 30s + serve stale prices

When Jupiter returns 429, the price service now backs off for 30s
instead of retrying every sweep. Stale cached prices (up to 2 min old)
are served while backed off so the monitor loop continues working.

Also reduced strategy log frequency — was logging every sweep for
tokens above 3.5x MCAP, now only every 20 sweeps.

修复API路由结构以匹配Vercel要求

创建正确的API端点文件结构：
- api/auth/login.js - 处理用户登录并返回JWT token
- api/auth/init.js - 初始化数据库表（users和settings）
- api/auth/ensure-users.js - 确保默认用户（GYDZ和STAFF）存在

这修复了405错误，因为之前的api/auth.js使用查询参数结构
(?action=login)，但Vercel需要文件路径匹配URL路径。

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Run backtest CPU work in thread pool to unblock event loop

Root cause: regime_engine.classify() and signal_engine.generate_signal()
are async def but purely CPU-bound (no await). When run via asyncio.create_task,
they block the entire FastAPI event loop for minutes, preventing other requests
and the timeout check from executing.

Fix:
- Split backtest into Phase 1 (async data fetch) and Phase 2 (CPU computation)
- Phase 2 runs via asyncio.to_thread() in a thread pool
- Uses dedicated asyncio.new_event_loop() for calling fake-async methods
- 5-minute timeout now actually works since it runs in its own thread
- DB session opened only for save (not during multi-minute computation)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
